title: Peacock Bar incident — written and rewritten the morning after
html:
TL;DR

Two passes at writing down what happened the night before at peacock bar. The first pass is the note as letter to self form — second person, present tense, raw and sprawling, processing in real time. The second pass (added later, modified through 2025-03-05) is the cleaned-up, third-person, lawyer-ready version: states the timeline plainly — chance encounter with a former partner after 10 months of no contact, an escalation involving punches, a taser, a bouncer, restraint, handcuffs, transparent answers to police, vitals checked, released. Yahir's later version explicitly frames his side as defensive and is careful with verbs.

This summary deliberately does not name the other parties involved. The raw note is preserved; Yahir's the only one who should choose how to surface it.

Why it matters in the graph

  • A clean before/after example of Yahir's two writing modes: the unfiltered first-person pass for processing, then a sanitized third-person rewrite when the stakes change. Worth tagging as a stylistic specimen even setting aside the content.
  • Falls into the broader post breakup conflict category and is a load-bearing event for understanding the 2025 mood (compare with 2025 09 23 how im scraping by six months later — financial and physical isolation continue).
  • Also evidence that Yahir uses Apple Notes for legal-grade documentation, not just brainstorming.

Open questions

  • Did this go anywhere legally? — possible later thread to find
  • Was this a trigger for the eventual move to austin tx (Sep 2025)?
